metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the three-way `enabled` override protocol in `produce_code_review_verdict`: `enabled=True` → skip the `code_review_enabled()` check and run; `enabled=False` → return `_inert_code_review_verdict()` immediately; `enabled=None` (default) → read config via `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` as today (confirm the `code_review_enabled()` utility function signature is NOT changed).
findings.finding: The `enabled: bool | None = None` override for `produce_code_review_verdict` is referenced as a deliverable but no processing protocol is specified for how it interacts with the existing `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` call — specifically whether `enabled=True` bypasses ONLY the config check or also affects the `code_review_enabled` utility function (which has no such parameter in its current signature at gate_dispatch.py:355).
findings.location: What section / AC3: 'produce_code_review_verdict gains an enabled: bool | None = None override'; gate_dispatch.py:392–423
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'The `enabled` override is applied as a short-circuit inside `produce_code_review_verdict` before the `code_review_enabled()` call: `if enabled is True: pass  # skip the check` / `if enabled is False: return _inert_code_review_verdict()` / `if enabled is None: if not code_review_enabled(repo_root): return _inert_code_review_verdict()`. The `code_review_enabled()` helper signature is unchanged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the 'What' section or vote mapping table whether `produce_code_review_verdict` can return an inert/disabled verdict when called with `enabled=True`, and if so under what condition — or explicitly label the inert-disabled row as a defensive/belt-and-suspenders mapping for future callers that do not pass `enabled=True`.
findings.finding: The vote mapping table includes 'PASS but `coverage.enabled==false` (inert) → BLOCK (gate-disabled)' as a live case requiring a test (AC2), yet the 'What' section says the adapter always passes `enabled=True` to force-enable the gate. If `enabled=True` reliably prevents an inert verdict, this table row is unreachable from the adapter's code path. The plan does not explain how an inert verdict could still be returned when `enabled=True` is supplied, leaving an implementer uncertain whether to write a defensive mapping (dead code) or whether there is a real scenario (e.g. a bug in `produce_code_review_verdict`) the mapping guards against.
findings.location: Vote mapping table / 'What' section (`enabled=True` override) / AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence note to the vote mapping table row for 'inert-disabled': e.g. 'Defensive guard: reachable if a future caller omits `enabled=True`, or if `produce_code_review_verdict` honors a secondary config override that `enabled=True` does not fully suppress (document which wins).' If `enabled=True` truly makes inert unreachable, label the row 'defensive dead-code, kept for belt-and-suspenders' so the test is understood as a contract test rather than a live path test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the adapter obtains the `LLMConfig` required as the first positional argument to `produce_code_review_verdict` — either state that `adapter.code_review_decision` must call `LLMConfig.from_env(repo_root=repo_root)` internally (adding `from rebar.llm.config import LLMConfig` to the adapter's lazy imports), or describe an alternative config-passing mechanism — so the implementer has an unambiguous wiring instruction.
findings.finding: The plan specifies calling `produce_code_review_verdict` directly from the adapter but never states how the adapter obtains the required `cfg` (LLMConfig) positional argument. The current function signature is `produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, *, base, head, diff_text, changed_files, runner, target_ticket, repo_root)` where `cfg` is an `LLMConfig`, not a `ReceiverConfig`. The adapter today only has access to `ReceiverConfig`. The plan's What/AC sections describe the return-shape changes and the `enabled` override but are silent on how the adapter constructs or imports the `LLMConfig` needed to make the call — a load-bearing implementation detail the implementer must not have to guess.
findings.location: ## What — 'Rewrite adapter.py's code_review_decision over gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict'; AC line 1; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:392 (signature: `def produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, *, ...)`)
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add: 'the adapter lazily imports `LLMConfig` from `rebar.llm.config` and calls `LLMConfig.from_env(repo_root=repo_root)` to construct the cfg argument — parallel to how shim.py does it (shim.py:138). The `repo_root` already flows in as a parameter so no new argument is needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the plan that the tagged message (`[LLM-Review: PASS]`, `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — finding]`, `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap (<sub-reason>)]`) is constructed entirely inside `adapter.py`'s `code_review_decision` and returned as the `message` field; `coverage_gap` is an additive boolean for test assertions and observability only — voter.py is NOT modified to read or use it.
findings.finding: The `coverage_gap` bool added to the adapter return dict is never consumed by `voter.py` (voter reads only `decision.get('decision')` and `decision.get('message')`), so the machine-parseable first-line tag must be embedded in the `message` field by the adapter itself. But the plan does not specify where the tag-construction logic lives: in the adapter (constructing the tagged message) vs. in the voter (reading coverage_gap and prepending the tag). This is an instruction-locality gap: the protocol for translating `coverage_gap + sub-reason → tagged message` has no co-located home in the plan.
findings.location: Distinct coverage-gap message section; AC4 ('vote message distinguishes a coverage-gap -1 … via the documented first-line tag'); voter.py:241

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task (or extend AC1) to update `adapter.py`'s docstring to reflect the completed reimplementation, removing the 'b744-WS6 will REIMPLEMENT' forward reference and replacing it with a description of the actual implemented behavior.
findings.finding: The `adapter.py` docstring — 'b744-WS6 will REIMPLEMENT this same signature over `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` — drop-in, with no caller change' — will become stale forward-reference text once WS6 ships, but no AC or plan task covers updating or removing it.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, append: 'The `adapter.py` module docstring is updated to describe the implemented four-pass gate integration, removing the WS6 forward-reference placeholder.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC1's 'return shape unchanged' with the plan's additive `coverage_gap` field: either update AC1 to say 'the caller-facing `{decision, message, findings}` shape is unchanged; `coverage_gap` is added as an optional field' or add a separate AC asserting `coverage_gap` is present and correctly populated in each verdict mapping case.
findings.finding: AC1 states the return shape is 'unchanged' but the plan body explicitly adds a `coverage_gap` flag as an additive field — these are contradictory, leaving it ambiguous whether `coverage_gap` is part of the verified return shape or not.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 vs 'What' section bullet 1
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC1 to: 'the signature + `{decision, message, findings}` return shape are unchanged (voter.py is not touched); the return dict additionally carries a `coverage_gap: bool` field whose value is tested in AC2 for each verdict branch.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) that explicitly verifies the old `rebar.llm.review_code` import and call are removed from `adapter.py`, and that `blocking_severities` is no longer applied on the four-pass code path (ruff/grep check or a test asserting the symbol is not imported).
findings.finding: No AC verifies removal of the old `review_code` call from `adapter.py` — the migration criterion covers replacement but not removal, leaving the legacy code path potentially alive alongside the new one.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `enabled=None` (the default) in the new `produce_code_review_verdict` signature falls through to `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` — preserving the shim.py call-site at `src/rebar/llm/code_review/shim.py:139` behavior exactly — and add a test or inline note that the shim's disabled path is unaffected by the new parameter.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `produce_code_review_verdict` will gain an `enabled: bool | None = None` parameter (WS6 AC3), but the existing shipped call-site at `src/rebar/llm/code_review/shim.py:139` invokes `produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, ..., repo_root=repo_root)` without any `enabled` kwarg. The plan does not verify that this existing call-site is backward-compatible with the new parameter (i.e., that `enabled=None` correctly falls through to `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` and does not alter shim.py's behavior).
findings.location: What section — 'Add an enabled: bool | None = None override to produce_code_review_verdict' / AC3 / src/rebar/llm/code_review/shim.py:139
findings.suggested_fix: In the `produce_code_review_verdict` implementation, replace the current `if not code_review_enabled(repo_root):` guard with `if enabled is False or (enabled is None and not code_review_enabled(repo_root)):` — so `enabled=True` forces run, `enabled=False` forces inert, and `enabled=None` (the default, used by shim.py) reads config as before. AC3's test should assert that a call with `enabled=None` and `verify.enable_code_review=false` still returns the inert verdict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out note to the plan (e.g., 'to revert: re-point `code_review_decision` to the single-pass `review_code` call; the voter and receiver are unchanged') or introduce a feature flag / config toggle that allows switching back to the old engine without a code revert.
findings.finding: The plan changes live Gerrit voting behavior (from single-pass LLM heuristic to four-pass gate) but provides no explicit rollback or back-out mechanism — deferring to WS6-b does not satisfy T4's requirement for an explicit rollback path on a behavioral change consumers rely on.
findings.location: Dependencies section, Scope section ('OUT: live end-to-end dogfood (WS6-b)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph 'Rollback' subsection under Dependencies: 'If the four-pass adapter causes unexpected voting behavior post-deploy, revert by restoring the single-pass `review_code` call in adapter.py (the voter and receiver are unaffected). A config toggle (`verify.review_engine: four-pass|single-pass`) can be added as a lower-risk alternative.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the sub-reason derivation precedence rule for mixed `security_detectors` lists: define whether a real `detector-finding` note takes precedence over a `fail-closed-abstain` note (i.e. 'if ANY note has reason==detector-finding → finding, not coverage-gap; else if ANY note has reason==fail-closed-abstain → scanner') and document this rule alongside the vote-mapping table.
findings.finding: The coverage-gap sub-reason derivation rule for the `scanner` case is ambiguous when `coverage['security_detectors']` contains a mix of `reason=='fail-closed-abstain'` notes and `reason=='detector-finding'` notes (e.g. one criterion abstained, another matched). The plan says 'a security_detectors note with reason==fail-closed-abstain → scanner' but does not specify: (a) whether any abstain note triggers scanner or only if ALL notes are abstains, and (b) how to classify the overall verdict when both a real finding and a fail-closed abstain are present.
findings.location: Distinct coverage-gap message section; 'coverage_gap sub-reason is derived deterministically from verdict.coverage'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Distinct coverage-gap message' section: 'Precedence when security_detectors contains both reasons: if ANY note has reason==detector-finding, the adapter treats the block as a real finding (coverage_gap=False, tag: BLOCK — finding); the fail-closed-abstain note(s) are surfaced in the message body but do not override the finding classification. coverage-gap(scanner) applies only when ALL blocking security_detector notes are fail-closed-abstain.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC (or an explicit cross-reference to a tracked WS6-b criterion) that represents an observable improvement for Gerrit submitters — e.g., a before/after comparison of vote accuracy on a recorded diff fixture, or an explicit statement that WS6-b's dogfood result is the acceptance gate for this plan.
findings.finding: Every AC in this plan is a system-internal assertion (tests pass, mypy clean, ADR written); the only concrete end-user validation mechanism — live dogfood of the four-pass verdict on real Gerrit changes — is explicitly deferred to WS6-b and excluded from scope, leaving no in-plan criterion that represents an observable improvement to the Gerrit submitter.
findings.location: Scope section ('OUT: the live end-to-end dogfood (WS6-b)') and Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs are test/static-analysis assertions)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A regression fixture of ≥3 known-bad diffs (one real finding, one infra-outage, one clean) produces the expected decision+tag under the four-pass adapter, demonstrating a behavioural improvement over the old single-pass path.' This can be tested offline (mock the gate) and gives reviewers a concrete before/after signal without requiring a live env.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
